assertion.title: Intra-evolution early stopping cuts runtime 96% (244s to 10s) while maintaining 50/50
assertion.statement: Adding early stopping within evolve() that terminates the generation loop when implied MSE (derived from fitness + parsimony penalty) drops below 1e-8 reduces total benchmark time from 244s to 10s (96% speedup) while maintaining perfect 50/50 exact matches across 3 consecutive runs. 29/50 equations solve at generation 0, 47/50 within 5 generations.

details.hypothesis: Since permutation templates seed exact solutions into the initial population, most equations have near-zero MSE from generation 0 but the system wastes 79 more generations of evolution. Early stopping when MSE reaches near-zero should dramatically cut runtime.
details.method: Modified evolve() to compute implied_mse = -(fitness + PARSIMONY_COEFF * tree_size) each generation. When implied_mse < 1e-8, the generation loop breaks immediately. This differs from the prior early-exit optimization (np_20260403_131855_early_exit) which stopped the RESTART loop, not the GENERATION loop.
details.changes: Added 5-line early stopping check inside the generation loop of evolve() in train.py. No other changes.
